title: 1995_The_Importance_of_the_Nitrous_in_NOx_modelling
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 1]
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

Key numbers, constants, parameters:
*   Fuel-air equivalence ratio: 0.5 to 0.7
*   NOx emission: 15 to 30 ppmv (15 percent O2, dry)
*   Nitrous oxide contribution: 40 to 45 percent of NOx for high-pressure engines (30 atm)
*   Nitrous oxide contribution: 20 to 35 percent of NOx for intermediate pressure engines (10 atm)
*   NOx less than 10 ppmv (15 percent O2, dry), the nitrous oxide contribution increases steeply and approaches 100 percent
* Pressure: 10 atm, 30 atm

title: 1995_The_Importance_of_the_Nitrous_in_NOx_modelling
text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   N + NO = N2 + O (1)
*   N + NO = N2 + O('D) (1a)
*   N + O2 = NO + O (2)
*   N + O2 = NO + O('D) (2a)
*   N + OH = NO + H (3)
*   N2O + M = N2 + O + M (4)
*   N2O + M = N2 + O('D) + M (4a)
*   N2O + O = NO + NO (5)
*   N2O + O = N2 + O2 (6)
*   N2O + H = N2 + OH (7)
*   N2O + H = NO + NH (8)
*   N2O + OH = N2 + HO2 (9)
*   N2O + CO = NO + NCO (10)
*   N2O + M = N + NO + M (11)
*   N₂ + CH = HCN + N (12)
*   NO + HO2 = NO2 + OH (13)

5.  **Key numbers, constants, parameters:**
    *   Pressure (P) = 0.92 atm (Jet-Stirred Reactor)
    *   Fuel-air equivalence ratio (phi) = 0.75  (Jet-Stirred Reactor)
    *   Reactant Inlet Temperature (Tin) = 295 K (Jet-Stirred Reactor)
    *   Inlet air humidity ratio = 8 x 10^-4 g H2O/g dry air  (Jet-Stirred Reactor)
    *   Mean residence time (tau) = 6 to 40 ms (Jet-Stirred Reactor)
    *   NOx yields =  <10 ppmv and modest (<100 ppmv) (Jet-Stirred Reactor)
    *   Reactor pressure is ambient = 1.0 or 0.92 atm  (Jet-Stirred Reactor)
    *   CH4 dissociation reaction (20 to 35 atm)
    *  long procedure just described (within 4 percent)
